articles.title: SURGING TRADE DEFICIT LIKELY TO REVERSE IN MONTHS AHEAD
articles.summary: The US trade deficit hit near-record levels in February (-$123B) as businesses rushed imports before new tariffs took effect. This surge is likely temporary - once tariffs are implemented, expect a big reversal as higher prices dampen demand for foreign goods. For startups, this economic shift presents both challenges and opportunities: supply chains will face disruption and imported components will become more expensive, potentially creating inflation pressure across sectors.

articles.title: DID WE JUST EXPERIENCE A "BEFORE, AND AFTER" DAY?
KLARNA AND STUBHUB DELAY IPOS
articles.summary: High-profile IPOs from fintech Klarna and ticket marketplace StubHub have been paused following market turmoil caused by Trump's new tariffs. Both startups aimed to raise over $1 billion but delayed investor roadshows as public markets tumbled.

articles.title: THE 25-DAY PLAN TO REVIVE CLOSED-LOST OPPORTUNITIES
articles.summary: Lost deals aren't dead forever - they're often just waiting for the right moment to reopen. First, understand why each opportunity died through CRM data or call recordings, then watch for triggers like new decision-makers, funding rounds, or changing business needs that make timing right. When ready, execute a structured 25-day outreach: start with day 1's triple-touch across LinkedIn, phone, and email, then progress through educational content about new features on day 6, expand to other buying committee members by day 8, send personalized video demos by day 15, and finish with an open-ended goodbye that keeps the door ajar for when they inevitably return to the market.

articles.title: SOMETIMES NEVER COMPETE ON PRICE
articles.summary: The difference between failing "low price" strategies and wildly successful ones like Amazon, Costco, Southwest, and Vanguard isn't just charging less - it's creating a comprehensive strategic system where "low price" emerges naturally from deliberate trade-offs. These companies didn't just cut margins. Instead, they built unique business models with intentional weaknesses that competitors couldn't copy.

articles.title: OFFENSIVE VS. DEFENSIVE DATA ANALYSIS
articles.summary: Most startups treat analytics like firefighting—only analyzing data when metrics break. Instead, founders should shift to proactive "offensive analysis," regularly identifying what's working, why, and how to amplify it, turning insights into strategic growth initiatives. To embed more “offense” into your culture, reserve dedicated team time weekly, elevate data teams as strategic partners, and intentionally celebrate proactive wins.

articles.summary: The Open Source AI Toolkit for TypeScript by Vercel.

articles.title: DIGITS AI ACCOUNTING (PRODUCT)
articles.summary: Digits is an AI-powered accounting platform with real-time insights, automated bookkeeping, and seamless bill pay.

articles.title: SKARBE (PRODUCT)
articles.summary: Skarbe is a CRM-free sales platform that automates deal tracking, follow-ups, and meeting insights.

articles.title: DISHING ON INVESTORS' FAVORITE ENTERPRISE STARTUPS WITH WING'S PETER WAGNER
articles.summary: The Enterprise Tech 30 list highlights Browserbase, Clay, Cursor, and Databricks as top companies. This discussion covers notable snubs, newcomers, and lessons from past lists. Check out the full conversation with Wing's Peter Wagner for detailed insights.

articles.title: GO DO BUSINESS
articles.summary: Business skills are honed through practical experience, not just by consuming content. You improve by actively hiring, building products, and engaging in business activities. Similar to learning music or sports, success in business requires hands-on practice to build muscle memory.

articles.title: NEXTHOP AI LOCKS UP $110M LED BY LIGHTSPEED
articles.summary: Nexthop AI has secured $110 million in funding led by Lightspeed Venture Partners, with participation from Kleiner Perkins and others. The company provides custom networking solutions and AI-powered network operating systems for hyperscalers. This funding round enables Nexthop AI to strengthen its partnerships with cloud companies to enhance infrastructure efficiency.

articles.title: FRENCH VC FIRM FOUNDERS FUTURE PLANS US EXPANSION
articles.summary: Paris-based VC firm Founders Future, which manages €300 million in assets, has sold a 25% stake in its holding company to investors like MACSF and the Dassault family.

articles.title: THE #1 THING THAT MAKES ENTERPRISE CUSTOMERS SEE VALUE
articles.summary: Your buyer's boss will never use your app, so how will they know it's worth the $100K they spent?

articles.title: SELLING DURING A DOWNTURN
articles.summary: This downturn may feel different from previous ones, but the winning strategy remains the same - identify specific patterns affecting your business, and improve what's in your control while accepting what isn't.
